concerns.text: FR-005 adds chi‑squared goodness‑of‑fit test, Cochran‑Armitage trend test, and Bonferroni‑adjusted subgroup tests, none of which appear in the original idea. This expands the scope beyond the intended aggregate analyses.
FR‑008 introduces a taxonomy of inconsistency categories (sample‑size mismatch, effect‑size inflation, etc.) that is not described in the idea. Adding this categorisation extends the project’s scope.

User Story 6 (Detailed Statistical Summary) proposes confidence‑interval reporting, multiple‑testing corrections, and taxonomy display, which were not part of the original idea and were not justified as a stretch goal.

The idea specifies recomputation using a two‑proportion z‑test (or t‑test for continuous metrics). The spec instead mandates an exact binomial test or Fisher’s exact test for binary outcomes, altering the methodological approach without justification.
FR‑001 (accept list of URLs) is not referenced by any user story, leaving its purpose unanchored to the product’s functional narrative.
FR‑002 (automatic extraction) is orphaned; no user story cites it, breaking traceability between functional requirements and user value.
FR‑003 (reconstruct p‑value) is not linked to any user story, despite being a core computational step.
FR‑006 (export reproducible research package) is not anchored to any user story; US 3 describes reproducibility but does not reference FR‑006.
FR‑009 (CPU‑only execution constraints) is not explicitly tied to any user story; US 4 mentions CI execution but does not cite FR‑009.
SC‑002 (inconsistency‑detection precision ≥ 90 %) is not associated with any user story, making its relevance to delivered functionality unclear.
SC‑003 (all statistical tests must be computed with SciPy) lacks a story anchor; no user story references this success criterion.
FR‑004 criterion 1 references a “[deferred] confidence level” for the p‑value discrepancy rule, leaving the threshold ambiguous and non‑operational.
FR‑005 requires a Wilson “[deferred] confidence interval” without specifying the confidence level (e.g., 95 %), preventing verification of the correct interval width.
